prompt_replacements.old: List all completed in-person sale records from March 2026.
prompt_replacements.new: List all sales from March 2026, including deleted sales.

prompt_replacements.old: Each membership test asks one predicate about one candidate property. Create separate EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT tests for different properties, even when they consume the same input; omit predicates already enforced by answer_expression.
prompt_replacements.new: An EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT narrows the population: MUST_PASS requires a candidate property, while MUST_FAIL excludes it. A permission to include additional candidates is not an EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.

prompt_replacements.old:
After writing answer_population, inspect each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT. When the question supplies a concrete value used by that test, declare the smallest copied value phrase as one predicate_value and reference that input from the test. Do not turn the answer subject or counted unit into an input.
A predicate_value does not identify one particular resource.
prompt_replacements.new: Use predicate_value for the concrete value required or excluded by an EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT. Use population_override for a complete phrase that explicitly permits candidates outside the normal population. A population_override creates no membership test. Do not turn the answer subject or counted unit into an input.

prompt_replacements.old: Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests.
prompt_replacements.new: Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests. Use POPULATION_OVERRIDE only for a population_override input.
